sanskrit:
नैव किञ्चित् करोमीति युक्तो मन्येत तत्त्ववित्।
पश्यञ्शृण्वन् स्पृशञ्जिघ्रन्नश्नन् गच्छन् स्वपन् श्वसन्॥
प्रलपन् विसृजन् गृह्णन्नुन्मिषन् निमिषन्नपि।
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेषु वर्तन्त इति धारयन्॥
transliteration: naiva kiñcit karomīti yukto manyeta tattva-vit | paśyañ śṛṇvan spṛśañ jighrann aśnan gacchan svapan śvasan || pralapan visṛjan gṛhṇann unmiṣan nimiṣann api | indriyāṇīndriyārtheṣu vartanta iti dhārayan ||
literal_meaning: The knower of Truth, established in yoga, thinks 'I do nothing at all.' While seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, walking, sleeping, breathing, talking, releasing, grasping, opening or closing the eyes, he holds that only the senses are moving among sense-objects.
contextual_meaning: A psychologically integrated person experiences life as pure witnessing awareness; bodily actions continue automatically without generating a false sense of personal agency or ownership.
psychological_state: Observing-ego position that metabolizes daily activities without self-referential rumination; akin to mindfulness-based 'decentering' where sensations, thoughts and actions are seen as transient events rather than identity.
trigger_scenario: You’re rushing through breakfast while mentally rehearsing a presentation; coffee spills, you reflexively wipe it, and suddenly notice you’ve been on autopilot—realizing the body handled everything while ‘you’ were absent.
anchor_text: Watch life happen; the body acts, you simply witness.
